QF.id: VIII Sales Practices and Investor Protection Services_13

question.text: When the IRRA platform is enabled for a trading member's clients, who is informed and how?

answer.text: All investors of the TM are informed by the exchange through email/SMS and a public notice on the exchange's website; the TM must also display on their website
Only the TM is informed; clients must check on their own
SEBI directly contacts each affected client via registered mail
Only clients with open positions are informed through the trading terminal

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When IRRA is enabled: (1) Exchange informs all investors of the TM via email/SMS and public notice on exchange website, (2) TM must display on their website, (3) Investors can then use IRRA to close positions and cancel orders. **The Trap:** The communication is multi-channel (email + SMS + website). Both exchange and TM have communication obligations. **Cross-Exam Context:** IRRA operational procedures are tested in Series VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IRRA notification: exchange (email/SMS/website) + TM (own website). All clients notified, not just those with positions.
